sections.paragraph:
οὑτοσί (Trag., Aristoph. et al.; Joseph.; Just., A II, 14, 1) a position described as relatively near rather than remote in present discourse, this (here) ἐπὶ ταυτησὶ τῆς γῆς here on this earth Papias (2:12).

Trag.

Trag. = writer (s) of tragedy

trag. = tragedy, writer (s) of tragedy

Aristoph Aristoph , V–IV b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

Joseph Joseph This abbr. used when no specific texts are cited (s. Jos.); I a.d.—List 5

Just Just , II a.d.—List 5

Papias Papias , early Christian writer, II a.d.—List 1

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 741
